content: We are getting flooded with bad reviews because of this and I am surprised there is no fix, or even a workaround since May 2021

Is this the same issue as this one? https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/32756

Personally I've only seen it coming from GCE phones (Google Cloud Engine), some kind of automated Google test probably. 

For those experiencing this on real devices, perhaps this Medium article might help: https://medium.com/codechai/flutter-app-couldnt-find-libflutter-so-c95ad81cbccd

Same issue flutter 2.10.1, detected by my users and crashlytics :

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/37028599/154852833-0ed291b8-705e-4977-bea2-e9d0b2c9aa00.png)

abiFilters will ban some device, be careful.
same issue here
I'm following

Try this workaround.

Add lines below to the end of file `settings.gradle`: 

```
gradle.beforeProject({ project->
    if (project.hasProperty("target-platform") &&
            !project.getProperty("target-platform").split(",").contains("android-arm")) {
        project.setProperty("target-platform", "android-arm,android-arm64")
    }
})
```

This will generate file `libflutter.so` on folders `x86_64`, `arm64-v8a` and `armeabi-v7a`. And for devices `x86`, them emulate arm.

The above workaround is abled to generate the libflutter.so file on the folders mentioned and I am able to install the app on emulator as well but again it blocking the app on some devices to install.

> The above workaround is abled to generate the libflutter.so file on the folders mentioned and I am able to install the app on emulator as well but again it blocking the app on some devices to install.

@bamakant you know what devices or specs of them?

> 



> Try this workaround.
> 
> Add lines below to the end of file `settings.gradle`:
> 
> ```
> gradle.beforeProject({ project->
>     if (project.hasProperty("target-platform") &&
>             !project.getProperty("target-platform").split(",").contains("android-arm")) {
>         project.setProperty("target-platform", "android-arm,android-arm64")
>     }
> })
> ```
> 
> This will generate file `libflutter.so` on folders `x86_64`, `arm64-v8a` and `armeabi-v7a`. And for devices `x86`, them emulate arm.

Thanks for this workaround, I am curious, do we still need to have abiFilters in build.gradle if we set this?

> > Try this workaround.
> > Add lines below to the end of file `settings.gradle`:
> > ```
> > gradle.beforeProject({ project->
> >     if (project.hasProperty("target-platform") &&
> >             !project.getProperty("target-platform").split(",").contains("android-arm")) {
> >         project.setProperty("target-platform", "android-arm,android-arm64")
> >     }
> > })
> > ```
> > 
> > 
> >     
> >       
> >     
> > 
> >       
> >     
> > 
> >     
> >   
> > This will generate file `libflutter.so` on folders `x86_64`, `arm64-v8a` and `armeabi-v7a`. And for devices `x86`, them emulate arm.
> 
> Thanks for this workaround, I am curious, do we still need to have abiFilters in build.gradle if we set this?

@vadrian89 you brought a good question. Not informing `abiFilters`, the folder `x86` is kept on build without `libflutter.so`, and possibly can trigger the error. But when we use both, the folder `x86` no longer appears and anothers continue with the file `libflutter.so`, and maybe forcing the device to load from `arm` folder.

Can anyone who has a real device that still with the problem test this theory?

The workaround and `abiFilters` below:

```
ndk {
  abiFilters 'armeabi-v7a', 'arm64-v8a', 'x86_64'
}
```

CC: @bamakant

> @vluizflavio It doesn't work on emulator if I add the filter. So I am guessing if it doesn't work on emulator it doesn't work on real device either.
> 
> The device emulated is a a Nexus 5X with Android 8.1, the device I have reports of crashing.

@vadrian89 Understood. I thought about it just as a way to try further filter the problem and find something in common, because the problem happen on specific devices. But can probably a useless test like you said :|

Some points below

The most of devices with the problem has:
CPU: Snapdragon
Architecture: 64-bit - ARMv8-A
GPU: Adreno

and a smaller percentage has:
CPU: MediaTek
Architecture: 64-bit - ARMv8-A
GPU: Mali

I made tests on devices with the same specs (architecture, CPU, GPU) and worked well on them. 

The OS version varies a lot on real devices, so I think this isn't related with the problem.

> we have same bug been 10 months or more. Anyone have updates? We tried all above attempts

I recently found this: Normally Flutter runs in JIT for faster compilation/debugging support in debug mode and AOT mode for better performance in profile and release mode. For platforms that Flutter cannot produce AOT artifacts for, such as Android x86 (32 bit), a JIT release build may be used instead. 

But I don't know if it can be useful or not.

and I found in documentation : When building your application in release mode, Flutter apps can be compiled for [armeabi-v7a](https://developer.android.com/ndk/guides/abis#v7a) (ARM 32-bit), [arm64-v8a](https://developer.android.com/ndk/guides/abis#arm64-v8a) (ARM 64-bit), and [x86-64](https://developer.android.com/ndk/guides/abis#86-64) (x86 64-bit). Flutter does not currently support building for x86 Android.
Same problem, this error popped up on my crashlytics. Nexus 5X Android 8.1.0
This is a severe scandal, disaster. Wtf is going on?
Any update on this serious issue?

> Same. And I dont get why people are closing the flutter issue without a full fix for almost a year.. Whats the motivation?
> 
> Anyways, This abifilters solution solved the issue on emulator blue stacks emulating an LG phone for me..
> 
> > android {
> > //...
> > defaultConfig {
> > ndk {
> > // Filter for architectures supported by Flutter.
> > abiFilters 'armeabi-v7a', 'arm64-v8a', 'x86_64'
> > }
> > }
> > }
> 
> Dont know if has drawbacks on other physical phones, but i will be updating this thread as we rollout and see which phones our customer's who get same issues again are getting it on

### Good news guys, so far after i published with both solutions , no customer of the company has received that error in our analytics so far.. A CLEAN Sweep,,

100.000+ people are using our app, so lets wait and see.. But so far, so very good for me combining both solutions..

By "second solution I've combined"
I mean a code that will generate file **libflutter.so** on folders x86_64, arm64-v8a and armeabi-v7a. And for devices x86, then emulate arm which are the devices with the problems..
Inside your **settings.gradle** file

> gradle.beforeProject({ project->
>     if (project.hasProperty("target-platform") &&
>             !project.getProperty("target-platform").split(",").contains("android-arm")) {
>         project.setProperty("target-platform", "android-arm,android-arm64")
>     }
> })


**Like earlier said, its all about Real devices.. so i could be having a "false positive" here..  But so far, so very good for 100,000+ physical phones with the app on playstore**  also its just been a month
